element_introduction_order.mastery_context: early enemy encounters with basic threats
keycard acquisition to disable exit energy fields
crystal shard acquisition alternative to hostage keycards
environmental threats requiring precision and avoidance
hidden progression path with loot-focused design

typical_tension_triggers: boss battles with persistent health bars across deaths
rhythm_pattern.pacing_descriptor: run-and-gun arcade progression with difficulty scaling tied to hostage rescue and boss encounter placement

spatial_grammar.optional_content_signal: hidden level gems discoverable within levels, leading to short secret areas
spatial_grammar.secret_convention: hidden gems trigger secret levels upon level completion; secret levels contain abundant pickups but may feature dense enemy/hazard clusters

difficulty_curve.early_game: foundational: basic platforming and default weapon combat in Episode 1 (Dr. Mangle's Lab shareware episode)
difficulty_curve.mid_game: escalation: hostage rescue objectives, weapon powerup introduction (machine gun, plasma), early boss encounters in Episode 2 (The Hidden Lab)
difficulty_curve.late_game: mastery: super gun availability, consistent boss encounters, instant-kill hazard density, Episode 3 (Master Cain) commercial conclusion
notable_spikes: first boss encounter (transition from pure platforming)
enemies requiring grenades/land mines/plasma (forcing weapon-specific mastery)

notable_level_patterns.description: Primary level completion gate: energy field blocks exit until keycard obtained (via hostage rescue) or crystal shard obtained (via boss kill). Creates dual-path objective structure.
notable_level_patterns.example_level: representative of most standard levels across all episodes

notable_level_patterns.description: Hidden gems embedded in level geometry; collecting triggers secret level access post-completion. Secret levels emphasize pickup abundance but may contain enemy/hazard-heavy variants.
notable_level_patterns.example_level: optional in each level, scattered across all three episodes

notable_level_patterns.description: Level state preserved at checkpoints; death returns player to checkpoint with enemy/boss health bars maintained, preventing reset-on-death cheese.
Certain enemies exclusively vulnerable to grenades, land mines, or plasma gun, forcing tactical weapon selection and ammo management.
